meta_title: For Students: analytics setup to Solve can't debug code (Intermediate Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome can't debug code. A Intermediate-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Analytics Setup to Debug Code Faster
content.seo_description: Stop guessing why your app fails. Learn how to implement advanced analytics to debug code effectively using Antigravity and Claude Code as a student.
content.answer_block: When you cannot debug your code, you lack visibility into runtime state. By implementing event-based analytics, you transform 'black box' errors into traceable data points. This approach allows you to pinpoint exactly where your logic fails, accelerating your development cycle from hours of guessing to minutes of surgical precision.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a blank console, heart rate climbing, while the feature you spent six hours building refuses to fire. It is the quintessential nightmare of every student developer: the code compiles, but the logic behaves like a ghost in the machine. You aren't just stuck; you are blind to the execution flow.

But the shift from 'junior' to 'engineer' happens when you stop guessing and start measuring. By instrumenting your application with surgical analytics, you turn runtime behavior into a searchable map. This guide will teach you how to leverage AI tools to build that map, transforming your debugging process from a frantic search into a systematic investigation.

steps.title: Define Instrumentation Points
steps.description: Identify the critical state transitions in your app where logic typically fails. We will focus on API handshakes and state mutations.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current codebase and suggest 5 high-impact locations for event logging that would help identify logic failures.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Identify locations for event tracking in src/services'

steps.title: Inject Analytics Middleware
steps.description: Create a centralized logging utility to ensure consistent data capture across the entire application stack.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a lightweight, type-safe analytics middleware that captures event name, payload, and timestamp for all API calls.

steps.title: Implement Error Boundary Tracking
steps.description: Capture the stack trace and state context whenever an unhandled exception occurs to stop guessing what caused the crash.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an error boundary component that sends the error stack and local state to our logging utility.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Add error boundary logging to App.tsx'

steps.title: Automate Log Aggregation
steps.description: Configure the output to pipe into an observability platform so you can visualize the timeline of events leading to a bug.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure an adapter to send our internal logs to a standard JSON-based logging endpoint.

steps.title: Querying for Patterns
steps.description: Use Claude Code to analyze your log output and look for recurring failure patterns or sequence anomalies.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review these log exports and find the sequence of events that consistently precedes a 500 error.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Analyze logs/error-log.json for failure patterns'

pro_tips: Log the 'before' and 'after' state of your objects, not just the error message.
Use unique correlation IDs for every user session to trace requests across asynchronous boundaries.
Don't log PII; ensure your analytics middleware scrubs sensitive data before transmission.
Set up 'heartbeat' events to detect if the app has frozen rather than crashed.
Adopt a 'log-first' development pattern where you define the telemetry before writing the feature logic.
content.next_steps: Once your analytics are live, force an error in your staging environment to verify that your logs capture the necessary state context. Then, integrate these insights into your PR process to ensure every new feature is debuggable from day one.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting your code and start mastering your architecture. Build the foundation that will power your future as a senior engineer. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
